title: Systematic Failures & Bias
concept: Systematic failures occur due to biases in the training data, such as background cues or texture over-reliance.

exercises.prompt: Recap: How does raising the detection threshold affect errors?

options.text: It decreases false positives and increases false negatives
It increases false positives and decreases false negatives
It decreases both false positives and false negatives simultaneously

exercises.explain: A higher threshold limits detections to high-confidence cases, reducing false alarms but increasing misses.

cards.title: The Husky vs. Wolf cheat
cards.body: In a famous study, researchers trained a model to classify wolves vs. huskies. It had near-perfect accuracy. But when tested on new images, it failed completely. Why? The model had simply learned that all images with snow in the background were 'wolves'.

cards.title: Systematic Bias
cards.body: A Systematic Failure occurs when a model makes repeated, predictable mistakes because it learned spurious correlations (biases) in the training data, such as background context, textures, or lighting.

cards.title: Memorizing the page numbers
cards.body: Imagine a student who notices that all the questions about geometry in their textbook happen to be on pages with blue headers. Instead of learning math, they just look for blue headers on the test. When given a real exam with no colors, they fail.

cards.title: Texture vs. Shape Bias
cards.body: Humans recognize a cat by its shape. CNNs, however, often rely on textures (fur pattern). If you overlay a cat texture on an elephant shape, the model will classify it as a cat, revealing a systematic texture bias.

cards.title: Models don't see like humans
cards.body: You might think a model sees a 'dog' because it understands eyes, legs, and tails. In reality, it sees local pixel textures and contrast lines. If all your training dogs are on grass, the grass is just as important to the model as the dog.

cards.title: Shape vs. Texture Focus
cards.body: Spurious cues that mislead neural representations:

left.text:
Abstract contour mapping.
Identifies elephant outline.
Robust to color/texture changes.

right.text:
Local pixel pattern matching.
Over-relies on skin/fur texture.
Easily fooled by texture overlays.

exercises.prompt: What is texture bias in convolutional networks?
exercises.explain: CNNs are highly sensitive to local textures (like fur or fabric patterns) and often prioritize them over structural outlines.

options.text: The tendency of CNNs to rely on local surface patterns rather than global shapes to identify objects
The model's inability to process colorful images
A hardware bug in GPU graphics memory rendering
A labeling error where textures are annotated in XML format

exercises.prompt: True or False: If a model learns to classify boats based on the presence of blue water in the background, it has learned a spurious correlation.
exercises.explain: True. Relying on the background water instead of the boat structure is a classic shortcut or spurious correlation.
Systematic errors are repeating patterns of failure caused by incorrect assumptions or training data biases.
exercises.sentence: A model making predictable, non-random mistakes due to biased training data is showing a ___ failure.

exercises.prompt: Which of these training strategies helps reduce background bias?
exercises.explain: Varying the backgrounds forces the model to ignore background pixels and focus on the object features.

options.text: Training on images with the target objects placed in diverse, mismatched backgrounds
Ensuring all images are shot under identical studio lighting
Removing the testing set from the computer entirely

exercises.prompt: You train a sheep detector. All training sheep are in green pastures. In production, the model misses sheep standing on rocky mountain terrain. How do you fix this systematic failure?

options.text: Collect and annotate images of sheep on diverse terrains (rocks, snow, roads) to force the model to decouple the sheep from pastures.
Train the model for double the epochs on the green pasture images.
Use a larger CNN architecture like VGG-19 on the same pasture data.
Change all rocky mountain images to green using Photoshop.

exercises.explain: The model has coupled the sheep concept with green grass. Adding diverse training terrains forces the network to learn the sheep's actual outline features.
